[2023-02-14 19:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): When I briefly thought about this, I felt that it may be possible that when aiming to establish a community where people can be comfortable and not exposed to negativity like "kys", immediate forced moderation might not be as effective as first trying to communicate a warning and going from there
[2023-02-14 19:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But I am not arguing for that view point with any data or knowledge, more just interested in ppl's opinions on why they would disagree
[2023-02-14 19:30] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): A warning _is_ moderation
[2023-02-14 19:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): When I say "forced moderation" I mean something that the person cannot do anything about -- i.e. getting forcefully muted
[2023-02-14 19:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): i mean you can argue that it hasn't been established that kys isn't acceptable, but given that cooledcannon has been banned, and kys is way worse, i think it's very clear-cut on that point
[2023-02-14 19:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Whereas a warning they CAN do something about -- they may be receptive and choose to change (moderate themselves)
[2023-02-14 19:31] awaclus: I actually think kys is quite a bit less severe than standard MoroN Hub level bantz
[2023-02-14 19:31] liquideggproduct (Liquid Egg Product): There are certain topics...suicide, murder, rape, etc., which should be relatively straightforward "unless you know for sure, these are red lines"
[2023-02-14 19:31] awaclus: like the fact that your nickname is MoroN is way worse than kys
[2023-02-14 19:31] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): Now i never met cooledcannon....but i'm pretty sure they did many persistin things worse than saying kys..
[2023-02-14 19:31] liquideggproduct (Liquid Egg Product): Hate to use "common sense", but insults based on literally ending one's life should cause pause
[2023-02-14 19:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): really? i don't think it is
[2023-02-14 19:32] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): What's MoroN Hub? is this prismata related?
[2023-02-14 19:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): and besides, many of the MoroN hub bantz is only acceptable because we know each other - i wouldn't want a stranger saying some of the things we say to each other
[2023-02-14 19:32] awaclus: it's a Prismata coaching community (at least on paper)
[2023-02-14 19:33] masn6811 (Masne98888): but in my opinion, it's very clear that telling someone kys is much more severe than calling someone a MoroN or punftard
[2023-02-14 19:34] awaclus: well it would be if you took it at face value, but the fact that it's a meme abbreviation makes it kind of hard to take it at face value
[2023-02-14 19:34] masn6811 (Masne98888): without any further context, it's not a meme abbreviation
[2023-02-14 19:34] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): The key here is consent. And blanket rules should be working of of a basis of no consent. Ofc we can all be dickheads to each other a once we decided it's funny. But community guidelines don't cover friends, they cover everyone
[2023-02-14 19:34] awaclus: how do you know what it stands for unless you are in a context where it is known to be a meme abbreviation
[2023-02-14 19:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I am not opposed to forced moderation for this offense, but I also feel like it's important to at least attempt to establish communication with the person

I'm not saying that in this case MasN would have to do that, but...

If we decide "we are OK with this person staying as part of the community and playing games, but only if they cannot chat" -- wouldn't it feel off if no communication ever happened to the person about their transgression?
[2023-02-14 19:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): im not asking for a perma-mute based on one comment - i think that would be over the top
[2023-02-14 19:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): im asking for a temp mute, accompanied by the reason for it
[2023-02-14 19:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Idk. You argue that "they should know it's not OK", and I agree with that -- but I still think voicing to the person that it's not OK is important
[2023-02-14 19:38] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): If they should know it's not okay
[2023-02-14 19:38] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): Then what's the point in telling them?
[2023-02-14 19:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): i think accompanying the mute with the reason *is* voicing that it's not OK
[2023-02-14 19:39] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Because humans are more complicated than machines
[2023-02-14 19:39] vanilvanil (Vanil - Ваніль): I think there's not much left to be said about this, unless a mod shows up and asks for opinions
[2023-02-14 19:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And there might be a chance that a bunch of shitty circumstances lead to them acting in a very bad way, but that they are open to working towards self-betterment and redemption (which are some pretty extreme ideas to apply to this scenario, but I'm trying to be more generic)
[2023-02-14 19:42] crash_overlord: I've warned flopflop to keep global chat civil. If he continues being overtly antagonistic, please ping the mod team again and we will escalate.

As far as the community guidelines of Prismata, KYS is against them. Any overtly antagonistic, harrasing or toxic language is against the community guidelines.
You can read the rules for this discord channel and the same would and does apply to in-game chat.
[2023-02-14 19:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But is the goal ONLY to make an example out of them, to work towards establishing a community where such behavior is not ok?

Or also to work towards this person recognizing their mistake and working on not repeating it (and not repeating similarly bad behavior)

> Here are some guidelines for good etiquette. Breaking these rules will likely make you seem impolite to other players. Serious violations could lead to consequences from our moderators.

> 
> Moderators will likely only issue a warning for mild violations of these rules, but repeated or belligerent violations could result in chat bans or other penalties if your bad manners are harming the Prismata community.

>     Don’t “offensively gg”. As the winning player, don’t say “gg” or make emotes of that nature before the game has ended. Most players consider it to be disrespectful.
>     Don’t balance-whine. Players tend to get annoyed if you complain excessively about Prismata units or game balance in the chats. A better place for that type of discussion is on the Prismata subreddit (where the devs are much more likely to respond).
>     Don’t argue in public. Keep heated arguments, flame wars, and excessive bitterness out of the Prismata chats, forums, and subreddit. They make you (and Prismata) look bad.
>     Don’t be a kibitzer. Kibitzing—offering unwelcome advice on games in progress—is highly frowned upon in tabletop games like chess and bridge. Many players find it extremely rude, so exercise restraint when commenting on in-progress games.
>     Don’t be divisive. Avoid goading or incendiary comments. Prismata is enjoyed by a diverse group of players of all cultures, beliefs, and skill levels. Avoid communicating in a manner that makes anyone feel unwelcome.
> 
> Moderators will likely only issue a warning for mild violations of these rules, but repeated or belligerent violations could result in chat bans or other penalties if your bad manners are harming the Prismata community.
